verses.text: And the Lord told Moses,
You must send people to go and look at the land of Canaan, the land that I gave to the Israelites. Send one person from each tribe, one of the leaders of the tribe.
Moses obeyed the command of the Lord and then sent people from the Paran Desert. They were all leaders of Israel
These are their names: from the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
From the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
From the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
From the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
From the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea son of Nun;
From the tribe of Benjamin. Palti son of Raphu;
From the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
From the tribe of Manasseh (a tribe from Joseph), Gaddi son of Susi;
From the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
From the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
From the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
From the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Maki.
These are the names of the men Moses sent to look at and study the land. (Moses called Hoshea son of Nun by another name. Moses called him Joshua.)
When Moses was sending them out to explore Canaan, he said, Go to the south into the hill country.
See what the land looks like. Learn about the people who live there. If they be strong or are they weak? Are they few or are they many?
Learn about the land that they live in. Is it good land or bad land? What kind of towns do they live in? Or do they live in camps. Do the towns have walls protecting them?

And learn other things about the land. Is the soil good for growing things, or is it poor soil? Are there trees on the land? Try to bring back some of the fruit from that land.” (This was during the time when the first grapes should be ripe.)
So they went to explore the country. They explored the area from the desert of Zin to Rehob and Lebo Hamath.

They entered the country through the south and went to Hebron. (The town of Hebron was built seven years before the town of Zoan in Egypt.) Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai lived there. These men were descendants of Anak.

Then the men went to Eshcol Valley. There they cut off a branch from a grapevine that had a bunch of grapes on it. They put that branch on a pole, and two men carried it between them. They also carried some pomegranates and figs
That place is called the Eshcol Valley, because there the men of Israel cut off the bunch of grapes
The men explored that country for 40 days, and then they went back to the camp

They came to Moses and Aaron and all the Israelites who came together at the town of Kadesh in the Paran Desert. The men told them what they had seen and showed them what the fruit of the land looked like.
They said to Moses: We went to the land that you sent us to. This is a land with a lot of food, milk, and honey. Here is some of the fruit of the land.
But there is a problem: The people who live in the land are strong. Their cities are very large and strongly defended. We even saw some Anakites there.
The Amalekites live in the south. The Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country. The Canaanites live near the sea and by the Jordan River.

Caleb told the people near Moses to be quiet. Then Caleb said, we should go up and take that land for ourselves. We can easily take that land. Numbers 13: 31 But the men who had gone with him said, we cannot fight against these people! They are much stronger than we are.

Then the other men said bad things to the Israelites about the land that they had seen. They said: we went through the land to look at everything. All the people that we saw there are very big. They will kill anyone who tries to attack them.
We saw giants (The descendants of Anak.) We felt like little grasshoppers. Yes, we were like grasshoppers to them!
That night all the Israelites in the camp began shouting loudly and crying.
The Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron and they said to them: We wish we had died in Egypt or in the desert.
Why did the Lord bring us to this land to die here in war? The enemies will take our wives and children. It will be better for us if we go back to Egypt.
They said to each other: Let us choose a new leader and go back to Egypt.
Moses and Aaron fell down with their faces to the ground in front of all the Israelites.
Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, were 2 of the men who had gone to see what the land was like. They tore their clothes
They said to the Israelites: We went through the land to see what the land was like, and we are telling you that this is a very good land.
If the Lord is pleased with us, He will bring us into this land and He will give it to us. This is land with a lot of food, milk and honey.

But you must not rebel against the LORD and you must not be afraid of the people in the land. We will easily destroy them. They have no protection, nothing to keep them safe. But we have the Lord with us, so don’t be afraid!
All of the Israelites began talking about killing Joshua and Caleb with stones. But then all of the Israelites saw the Glory of the Lord appeared over the meeting tent.
The Lord said to Moses: How long will these people continue to make me angry? They show that they don’t trust me or believe in my power, I have done many miracles for them.
I will send a disease and kill all of them. I will destroy them, and I will use you to make another nation. Your nation will be greater and stronger than these people.
Then Moses said to the Lord: If you do that, the Egyptians will hear about it. (They know that you used your great power to bring your people out of Egypt.)

The people of Egypt will talk to the people who live in this land. They will tell them that You, the Lord, were with your people and that You appeared to them. They will tell them that You led them with a cloud above them in the day and with fire at night.
If you kill your people now, then all the people will say:
The Lord was not able to bring them into the land he promised them. So, he killed them in the desert.
Lord, show your strength! Show it the way you said you would.

The Lord is slow to become angry. He is full of great love. He forgives those who are guilty and break the law. But he always punishes those who are guilty. He punishes them, and he also punishes their children, their grandchildren, and even their great-grandchildren.
Now, show your great love to these people. Forgive their sin. Forgive them the same way you have been forgiving them since the time they left Egypt until now.
The Lord said to Moses: I will forgive them because you have asked Me.
But I tell you the truth. As I live, the Glory of the Lord will fill the whole earth.
These people have saw my glory and the great signs that I did in Egypt and in the desert. But they disobeyed me and tested me ten times.
That is why none of them will see the land that I have promised their grandfathers. Not one of the people who have turned away from Me and did not want to trust Me will go into the land.
But my servant Caleb is different. He follows me completely. So, I will bring him into the land that he has already seen, and his people will get that land.
(The Amalekite people and the Canaanites live in the valley.) So tomorrow you must leave this place. Go back to the desert on the road to the Red Sea.
The Lord talked to Moses and Aaron and He said:
How long will these evil people continue to complain against me? I have heard their complaints.
So tell them, The Lord said that as HE surely lives that he will surely do all those things to you that you complained about. This is what will happen to you:
Your dead bodies will lie in the sand of this desert, all of you whose names are written in the book, all who are older than 20 years, all who have accused Me.
I promised that you would live in that land, but now you will not go there. Only Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, will go into the land.
You were afraid and complained that your enemies in that new land would take your children away from you. I tell you; I will bring them into the land. They will enjoy what you refused to accept.
Your dead bodies will lie in this desert,
Your children will wander around like shepherds here in the desert for 40 years. They will suffer because you were not faithful to me. They must suffer until all of you lie dead in the desert.

For 40 years you will suffer for your sins. (That is one year for each of the 40 days that the men explored the land.) You will suffer for 40 years for your sin, and you will know that I am angry with you and break my promise.
I, the Lord, have spoken. I will do this to these sinful people who have rebelled against Me. In this desert. You will all die.
The men Moses sent to explore the new land were the ones who came back and made all the Israelites complaining against Moses by bringing out a bad report about the land.
The men who had told those bad things to the other people all died of the disease that the LORD sent.
Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, had also gone with the men to look at the land, but they did not die. They kept on living.
When Moses told the Israelites everything that the Lord had said, the people were very sad and cried.
They got up early the next morning and went to the top of the mountain. They said: We have sinned. We will go to the place that the Lord promised us.
Moses said to them: Why are you not obeying the Lord’s command? You will not be successful!
Don’t go, because the Lord is not with you. If you go into the land, the enemies will defeat you.
The Amalekite people and the Canaanites were there before you. You will die in war because you have turned away from the Lord. He will not be with you, and He will not help you.
But the people did not believe Moses. They went toward the high hill country. But Moses and covenant box of the Lord did not go with the people.
The Amalekite people and the Canaanites who lived in those mountains came down and defeated the Israelites. They chased the Israelites who ran away and fled to as far as the town of Hormah.
Now Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took men:
And they rose up against Moses. With them were 250 Israelite men, well-known community leaders, famous in the church and some mighty men.

They came together to rebel against Moses and Aaron and said to them: ‘You want to do everything yourselves. To make sure everyone in the nation is holy and the Lord is with them. Why do you think that you are better then the people of Lord?
When Moses heard this, he bowed down and lay with his face to the ground.

Then Moses said to Korah and all his followers, “Tomorrow morning the Lord will show who belongs to him. He will show who is holy, and he will bring that man near to him. He will choose him and bring that man near to him.
Korah, you and all your followers must prepare your firepan for offering.
Tomorrow put fire and incense in some pans. Then bring those pans before the Lord. The man whom the LORD chooses shall be the one who is holy. You have gone far enough, you sons of Levi.
Moses also said to Korah, “You Levites, listen to me.

The God of Israel has chosen you from all the Israelites to be his special people, to serve Him in the tent of the Lord for the people of God. This is a very important work; don’t you think so? Numbers 16: 10 He brought you Levites near to him to help the priests, but now you are trying to become priests also. Numbers 16: 11 You and your followers have joined together and turned against the Lord! Did Aaron do anything wrong? No, so why are you complaining against Aaron?”
Then Moses called Korah, Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, to come to him, but they said: ‘We will not come.
Is it not enough that you brought us out of Egypt, a land flowing with milk and honey, you brought us to the desert to kill us. And now you want to show that you have even more power over us.

Why should we follow you? You did not bring us into the new land filled with many good things. You did not give us the land God promised. You did not give us the fields or the vineyards. Are you trying to fool these men? No! We will not come.
Moses became very angry. He said to the Lord: ‘Please do not accept the offerings that they want to give You. I have taken nothing from them, and I have not done anything wrong to them.’
Then Moses said to Korah: ‘Tomorrow you and Aaron and all the people with you will stand before the Lord.
Each of you must have a pan with incense in it to give to the Lord. Each of you must have your own pan. There will be 250 pans and you and Aaron must each have a pan.’
The next morning everyone took his pan with coals and put incense on it. They went to the entrance of the tent of the Lord, and they stood there, with Moses and Aaron.
Korah also gathered all the people together at the entrance of the tabernacle. Then the Glory of the Lord appeared to everyone there.20Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
Go away from these people. I want to kill them all immediately.
But Moses and Aaron bowed down and fell with their faces to the ground and said: ‘O God, you give life to everyone. It is only a few who have sinned. Why are You angry with all your people?’
Then the Lord said to Moses:
Tell the people to go away from Korah, Dathan and Abiram’s tents.
Moses went to Dathan and Abiram and the leaders of Israel went with him.

Moses warned the people and said: Please, go away from the tents of these bad people, and do not touch anything that belongs to them, so that you will not be destroyed because of the sin they have done.
The people went away from the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram came and stood at the doors of their tents with their wives and children and their other people.
Then Moses said: ‘Now you will know that the Lord has sent me to do all these things, and you will know that it was not I who decided them.
These men will die, but if they die in a normal way the way people always die then that will show that the Lord did not really send me.

But if the Lord makes something happen that is different, if the earth opens its mouth and swallows them with everything they have, if they go down alive into the underworld, then you will know that these people have sinned and turned away from God.’
When Moses finished saying these things, the ground under the men opened.
The earth opened its mouth and swallowed Korah and all the people with him and their families and everything that belonged to them.
They went down into their grave alive. Everything they owned went with them. Then the earth closed over them. They disappeared from the people of God.
The Israelites heard the cries of the men being destroyed. So, they all ran in different directions and said, the earth will swallow us too!
Then a fire came from the Lord and destroyed the 250 men who were offering the incense.
After so many Israelites had died, the Lord talked to Moses and Eleazar, the priest, son of Aaron, and He said:
You must count all the Israelites, everyone who is 20 years old or older, and anyone who can go to war. You must also write down from what families they come.
Then Moses and Eleazar, the priest, talked to the Israelites in the Moab Flatlands next to the Jordan River near the city of Jericho. They told them
List all the men of Israel 20 years old and older, just as the LORD commanded Moses. This is the record of all the families of Israel who came out of Egypt.
Reuben was the oldest son of Israel. The families in the Reuben tribe were Hanok, Pallu,
6 Hezron from the Hezronite family group; Carmi from the Carmite family.
There were 43,730 people in the families of the Reuben tribe.
The son of Pallu was Eliab,

The sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan and Abiram. The people had chosen Dathan and Abiram to be leaders. They were the people who rebelled against Moses and Aaron with the group of Korah. They were also rebellious against the Lord.
Then the earth opened and swallowed them with Korah. The Korah group died when 250 of their men were burnt. They became a sign to the other people,
But the children of Korah did not die.
The families in the Simeon tribe were: Nemuel, Jamin, Jakin,
Zerah from the Zerahite family; Shaul from the Shaulite family.
There were 22,200 people in the families of the Simeon tribe.
The families in the Gad tribe were: Zephon, Haggi, Shuni,
Ozni from the Oznite family; Eri from the Erite family;
Arodi from the Arodite family; Areli from the Arelite family.
There were 40,500 people in the families of the Gad tribe.
Er and Onan were sons of Judah, but they died in Canaan.
The families of the Judah tribe were: Shelah, Perez and Zerah.
The families of the descendants of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
There were 76,500 people in the families of the Judah tribe.
The families of the Issachar tribe were Tola, Puah,
Jashub from the Jashubite family; Shimron from the Shimronite family.
There were 64,300 people in the families of the Issachar tribe.
The families of the Zebulun tribe were: Sered, Elon and Jahleel.
There were 60,500 people in the families of the Zebulun tribe.
Joseph was the forefather of the families of Manasseh and Ephraim.
The first family in the Manasseh tribe was Makir and the people of Makir were the forefathers of the Gilead families.
The Gilead families were: Gilead, Iezer, Helek,
Asriel from the Asrielite family; Shechem from the Shechemite family;
Shemida from the Shemidaite family; Hepher from the Hepherite family.
Zelophehad, son of Hepher, had no sons. He had only daughters. The names of the daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milkah and Tirzah.
There were 52,700 people in the families of Manasseh. 35The families of Ephraim were Shuthelah, Beker and Tahan.
Shuthelah was also the forefather of the Eran family.
There were 32,500 people in the families of the Ephraim tribe. These were all the families who were descendants of Joseph.
The families of the Benjamin tribe were: Bela, Ashbel, Ahiram,
Shupham from the Shuphamite family; Hupham from the Huphamite family.
Bela was the forefather of the Ard and Naaman families.
There were 45,600 people in the families of the Benjamin tribe.
The Shuham family was the only family of the Dan tribe.
There were 64,400 people in the family of Shuham.
The families of the Asher tribe were Imnah, Ishvi and Beriah.
Beriah was the forefather of the families of Heber and Malkiel.
Asher also had a daughter whose name was Serah.
There were 53,400 people in the families of the Asher tribe.
The families of the Naphtali tribe were Jahzeel, Guni,
Jezer from the Jezerite family; Shillem from the Shillemite family.
There were 45,400 people in the families of the Naphtali tribe.
All of the Israelites together were 601,730.
The Lord said to Moses:
You must divide the land among these families. Each family must get as much land as the number of people in the family.
You must give a big family a bigger part of the land and a small family a smaller part of the land. Each family must get as much land as the number of people in the family.
But you must draw lots when you divide the land.
The lots will show what part of land each family must get, what land the big families must get and what land the small families must get.
The families in the Levi tribe were Gershon, Kohath and Merari.
There were also other Levi families. They were Libni, Hebron, Mahli, Mushi and Korah. Kohath was the father of Amram,
Amram married Jochebed. She was born in the land of Egypt, and she was a descendant of Levi. She and Amram had 3 children. They were Aaron, Moses and their sister Miriam.
Aaron was the father of Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
But Nadab and Abihu died when they sacrificed offerings to the Lord with fire that the Lord did not want.
There were 23,000 men in the Levi families who were one month old or older. Moses and Eleazar did not count them with the other Israelites because they did not get land with the other Israelites.
Moses and Eleazar the priest counted all these people. They counted the Israelites while they were in the Jordan Valley in Moab. This was near the Jordan River across from Jericho.
Many years before, in the desert of Sinai, Moses and Aaron the priest counted the Israelites. But all these people were dead. Not one of them was still alive,

The Lord had told those Israelites that they would all die in the desert, that was sure. Not one of those Israelites stayed alive. Only Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, were still alive.
